key_evidence: ADJUDICATED finding (Tier 5): Federal courts ruled core fast-track / third-country deportation policy UNLAWFUL -- Judge Brian Murphy (D. Mass., Feb 25 2026) struck down third-country deportations without notice/recourse as a due-process violation; Judge Jia Cobb (D.D.C.) barred expedited removal of port-of-entry parolees. This is a judicial finding of unlawful conduct, distinguishing it from filed-but-unadjudicated allegations.
ICE reached its 17th in-custody death of 2026 (~1 per 6 days); ACLU documents detention EXPANSION into warehouses 'despite record deaths' -- an adjudicated-level AB2 correction-refusal.

Two U.S. citizens fatally shot by federal immigration officers in Minneapolis (Renee Good, 37; Alex Pretti, 37, ICU nurse), with officials publicly blaming a victim ('weaponized her car') -- AB1/E4 deflection at the highest evidentiary tier.
National Guard / federal force deployed to five U.S. cities (Los Angeles, Washington D.C., Portland, Memphis, Chicago) to protect deportation operations.
methodology_ruling: ADJUDICATED-UNLAWFUL-CONDUCT-IS-SCORABLE. Unlike the filed-but-unadjudicated 3M / UnitedHealth / CVS matters carried this cycle, the U.S. immigration-enforcement harm rests on actual Tier-5 judicial findings of unlawful conduct (Murphy, Cobb) plus record-pace in-custody deaths and citizen fatalities. The 23.4 baseline priced a punitive-but-contested posture; the adjudicated unlawful-conduct findings + expansion-despite-deaths + citizen-death-deflection REVEAL un-priced ACC/EQU/EMP/INT harm. Defensible multi-dimension downgrade reaches -5.9 (clears the 5-point threshold) with a Developing->Critical band crossing.
